action.reasoning: Observation: resume still fails because architecture inference is choosing the wrong class for cam_unet_v3; stderr lists current-architecture keys as missing, meaning the legacy checkpoint is being loaded into the new model. Analysis: the fix is to make architecture detection deterministic from actual key patterns (legacy checkpoints have inc./down1.conv./up1.conv. keys; new checkpoints have in_proj/down1.block./up1.conv. but no inc.) and ensure both train.py and predict.py use the same loader. Decision: patch model.py/train.py/predict.py with explicit key-pattern detection and dual-model factory, then rerun the short fine-tune from cam_unet_v3, smoke test locally, and if successful submit next iteration.
action.command: python train.py --resume checkpoints/cam_unet_v3.pt --output checkpoints/cam_unet_v4.pt --epochs 8 --lr 5e-5 --batch_size 16 --num_workers 4 && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/cam_unet_v4.pt --output local_cam_v4_val_predictions.json
